identity.notes: Steve is an avatar/default skin, so identity analysis differs from authored narrative characters.

roleAndNarrative.notes: Story/personality fields are intentionally thin because Steve functions mostly as a player avatar.

visualDesign.silhouette: block-shaped avatar with square head and rectangular limbs
visualDesign.notes: Steve is a great test for avatar characters where silhouette and game style matter more than personality.

popularitySignals.notes: April 2025 spike is large and needs explanation before conclusions.

popularitySignals.value: Iconic as an avatar / Mainstream as a character
popularitySignals.notes: Steve's recognizability is tied heavily to Minecraft's player base and block style.

communityAndFanbase.notes: Avatar characters need different metrics from authored protagonists: player adoption and game reach matter more.

analysisUsefulness: Best pilot character for avatar/player-character type.
Strong contrast to Mario and Pikachu because Steve has little fixed personality or narrative arc.
Useful for testing whether visual/game-reach data can stand in for story data.
dataGaps: Need exact official Steve/Alex source citation.
Need explanation for April 2025 pageview spike.

normalizedCandidate.name: Steve
normalizedCandidate.summary: Minecraft's default player avatar, recognized less through story and more through blocky proportions, simple clothing, and the huge reach of Minecraft itself.

popularitySignals.notes: Normalized against Mario's current pilot total only for temporary comparison.

visualTags.silhouette: block-shaped avatar with square head and rectangular limbs

analysisNotes: Use Steve to test avatar-specific fields.
Story/personality fields should be nullable or low-weighted for avatar records.
